title: NH mom running Boston Marathon for Spaulding after son’s friend injured at beach
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jessica O'Brien from Milford, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
O'Brien's son Colin's friend Aiden Sloan was injured at Hampton Beach, receiving care from Spaulding Hospital.
Funds raised by O'Brien will support Spaulding's patient care and rehabilitation treatments.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston news.
The quiz covers trending stories from the Greater Boston area as of March 13, 2026.
General Assignment Editor Peter Chianca has been with Boston.com since 2019.

title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agreed to produce voting reports for jailed individuals after a lawsuit by Lawyers for Civil Rights.
The VOTES Act requires reports on incarcerated voters, but Galvin's office missed deadlines following the 2024 and 2025 elections.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for delays and committed to process improvements before the September statewide primary.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam, former Buffalo Bills fullback, signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to former NFL MVP Josh Allen in terms of potential.
Gilliam aims to enhance the Patriots' running game, drawing from his experience supporting top rushers at the Bills.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez will miss the first two months of the 2026 season following left shoulder surgery.
The Red Sox signed veteran lefty Danny Coulombe, who had a 2.30 ERA in 2025, to a one-year deal.
Gonzalez's injury prompted Boston to add infield depth with players like Isiah Kiner-Falefa and Andruw Monasteri.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a high school senior, is charged with murdering Janet Swallow, 68, at her Danvers home in a random attack.
Essex County DA stated there is no known connection between DeMayo and Swallow, and DeMayo is believed to have acted alone.
DeMayo was found with a bloody knife in Lynn, leading to the discovery of Swallow's death at her Amherst Street home.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are searching for a suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at a laundromat on Warren St in Roxbury.
The suspect reportedly kicked the 5-months-pregnant victim in the stomach after an argument escalated over a payment issue.
Police issued a description of the suspect and are requesting public assistance, offering a tip line for anonymous information.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer by flashing blue lights.
Beltran, formerly of Lawrence police, was terminated in March 2025 and is no longer a certified law enforcement officer.
Beltran was previously accused of firearm misconduct, sexual remarks to a minor, and assault, leading to his firing.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots may acquire Eagles' wideout A.J. Brown, depending on Philadelphia lowering their current high asking price.
NFL insider Mike Giardi predicts potential A.J. Brown trade to Patriots after Eagles reduce current demand.
Trading A.J. Brown after June 1 could minimize Eagles' dead cap hit from $43 million to $16.4 million.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck caught fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough around 8 a.m., affecting westbound lanes.
Firefighters from Westborough and Grafton extinguished the fire by 8:30 a.m.; lane closures lasted until 1:30 p.m.
No injuries were reported, and the cause of the fire is under investigation by local officials.
